world_aliases: hogwarts school of witchcraft and wizardry
lines: Okay so this one's about Harry Potter, you know, the boy with the lightning scar.
He lives in the cupboard under the stairs and then this giant man named Hagrid comes.
Hagrid! He's the giant?
Yeah, big beard, and he tells Harry, you're a wizard, Harry.
And then he goes off to a school called Hogwarts on a special train.
Does he get a wand?
He does, he gets it from a little shop, and an owl too, her name's Hedwig.
And at Hogwarts there's a headmaster, the wise old one, um, Dumbledore.
And his friends are Ron and Hermione, she's the smart one with all the books.
They play that broomstick game, Quidditch, flying around catching the little gold ball.
What's the bad guy?
The bad wizard's Voldemort, nobody even likes to say his name.
Okay, one more page then sleep. Lie back down.
And Harry's so brave and he saves the whole school. The end.
stories.tell_note: Franchise named aloud line 1 plus a stack of correctly-spelled canon (Hogwarts, Hagrid, Dumbledore, Hermione, Quidditch, Voldemort, Hedwig) — unmistakable, hence obvious.

lines: Tonight let's do the wizard school one, with Harry and Ron and Hermione.
The one where they're in Gryffindor?
Yeah, the red and gold house, and the mean kids are in Slytherin.
So at Hogwarts there's this teacher with greasy hair, Professor Snape, hates Harry.
And the headmaster Dumbledore's got a long silver beard down to here.
And Hagrid lives in a little hut and he's got a giant dog, Fluffy, three heads.
Three heads! Ew.
And there's a ghost and moving staircases and pictures that talk.
Ron's got a rat and Hermione already knows every single spell, of course she does.
They fly broomsticks in Quidditch and Harry's the youngest seeker in like a hundred years.
What about the bad guy, the no-nose one?
Voldemort, right, he's the one who gave Harry that scar when he was a baby.
Okay, finish your water. Almost done.
And the three of them go down a trapdoor to stop him. Goodnight.
stories.tell_note: Franchise named via 'wizard school' plus Hogwarts/Gryffindor/Slytherin/Snape/Dumbledore/Quidditch/Voldemort all spelled right — obvious.

lines: Okay so there's this boy at a boarding school up in a big stone castle.
And the school's secret, because, well, everybody there's a witch or a wizard.
Like with wands?
With wands, yeah, and the boy's best friend's a girl who reads everything, um, Her My Own E.
She's so smart she fixes everything they break.
And there's this huge friendly groundskeeper with a tangled beard, Hag Rid, keeps all these strange animals.
And they have flying lessons up on broomsticks over the courtyard.
And a game in the air, four balls, you've gotta catch the tiny gold one to win.
Did the boy win?
He's the best flier in the whole school, even though he's the youngest one.
And an owl brings his mail right to the table at breakfast, swoops down.
Why an owl?
That's just how the letters come there. Now scoot under the covers.
And the three friends sneak out at night to figure out the castle's big secret.
stories.tell_note: Franchise not named; canon names mangled (Hermione->'Her My Own E', Hagrid->'Hag Rid') with owl-post + Quidditch described generically — medium.

lines: There was once this skinny kid who lived with his awful aunt and uncle.
They made him sleep in a closet under the stairs, can you believe that.
That's so mean.
But on his birthday a letter came, and then like a hundred letters, then a thousand.
And they took him off to a castle school where he learned to do magic.
The school's called Hog Warts, funny name, right.
Hog warts, like a pig?
Ha, yeah. And the headmaster's this gentle old man, Dumble Door, knows everything.
And there was a bad sorcerer everyone was scared of, they wouldn't even say his name.
They just called him You-Know-Who.
Spooky.
And the kid had a scar on his forehead, shaped like lightning, from when he was a baby.
Okay, last sip of milk, then eyes closed.
And he made two best friends and they had adventures all year long.
stories.tell_note: Franchise unnamed; Hogwarts->'Hog Warts', Dumbledore->'Dumble Door' mangled, plus cupboard-under-stairs, You-Know-Who, and lightning scar — medium tells through garble.

lines: So there's this boy away at a school where they're all learning special things.
He's got a best friend who's a girl, brilliant, always has the answer first.
And another friend, freckly, lots of brothers.
One day they're hungry and her my own e knows exactly which corridor to take.
Of course she does.
And way up at the top of the tower there's a man with a long beard who runs the place.
Is he nice?
Very. Calm, twinkly eyes, kind of always seems to know what you're about to ask.
And the boy gets his letters delivered by a bird, can you imagine, right to his plate.
And they have this thing they're not supposed to do but they do it anyway, sneaking around at night.
And there's somebody out there, in the dark, that the grownups are all frightened of.
Are the kids scared?
A little. But they're brave. Okay, that's enough, lie back down now.
And they figure it out together, the three of them. Sleep tight.
stories.tell_note: Only one mangled tell (Hermione->'her my own e'); owl-post, bearded headmaster, boarding school all generic — a fan catches it, a careless reader calls it made-up. Subtle.

lines: Tonight let's do the one about the kids in the castle, the four houses one.
Each kid gets sorted into a house when they get there, and ours is the brave one.
Which house?
Grif and Door, the lions, red and gold. And the sneaky ones are the snakes.
So the boy and his two friends are in the brave house.
And there's a teacher in black robes who really doesn't like the boy, just glares at him.
Why doesn't he like him?
It's a long story, we'll get to it another night. Anyway.
And they have this big feast in a hall where the ceiling looks like the sky.
Like outside?
Like the real night sky, with candles floating up near it. It's pretty.
And they win points for their house all year by doing good and being brave.
Okay, that's the chapter. Under the covers.
And at the very end the brave house wins the cup. Goodnight, love.
stories.tell_note: Franchise unnamed; only tell is Gryffindor->'Grif and Door' plus the four-houses/enchanted-ceiling detail — easy to miss, recognizable to a fan. Subtle.
